Sub-problem 1: The director has planned scenes inspired by 5 different iconic Akshay Kumar movies. Each scene requires a different number of shooting days, specifically: 4, 6, 8, 10, and 12 days respectively. To optimize the schedule, the director wants to shoot these scenes in such a way that the total number of shooting days is minimized while ensuring that no two consecutive scenes come from movies released in the same decade. The decades are {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, and {2010-2019}. Given the release years of the movies as 1994, 2004, 2007, 2012, and 2015, the optimal shooting schedule is to shoot scenes in the order 10 (2010-2019), 6 (2000-2009), 4 (1990-1999), 8 (2000-2009), 12 (2010-2019). This arrangement ensures no two consecutive scenes are from the same decade and minimizes the total number of shooting days to 30 days.******

Sub-problem 2: The director has a total budget of ₹50 lakhs for the tribute film. The cost per day for shooting each scene is proportional to the complexity of the scene and the iconic status of the Akshay Kumar movie it represents. The costs per day for the scenes are ₹1 lakh, ₹1.5 lakhs, ₹2 lakhs, ₹2.5 lakhs, and ₹3 lakhs respectively. To maximize the number of completed scenes without exceeding the budget of ₹50 lakhs, the director should shoot the scenes in the order of lowest cost per day first. The optimal solution is to shoot the first scene (4 days at ₹1 lakh/day) for 4 days, the second scene (6 days at ₹1.5 lakh/day) for 6 days, the third scene (8 days at ₹2 lakh/day) for 8 days, the fourth scene (10 days at ₹2.5 lakh/day) for 10 days, and the fifth scene (12 days at ₹3 lakh/day) for 12 days.
